surface.name: Authenticate Snyk CLI and run the Ionic quick test
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Authenticate Snyk CLI and run the Ionic quick test. The scan-product router has no default, but the official CLI getting-started guide provides a self-contained quick test whose terminal output is a vulnerability report. Prior surface before policy commit: Snyk scan product and integration route not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: Snyk CLI quick test
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This route explicitly ends with a vulnerability report, but the general page presents CLI as an alternative for scanning without repository authentication.

alternatives_considered.name: SCM repository import
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This route scans an imported Project and displays results, but its integration provider, permissions, automation, and scan product depend on developer context.

entry_point.developer_intent: Reach the first documented security scan result on Snyk without a preselected product or integration.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No Snyk product, source system, integration method, or scan target has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The Snyk CLI getting-started guide ends when the terminal test prints the vulnerability issue report and its remediation guidance.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The terminal displays the issue report and its remediation guidance.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The terminal displays the issue report and its remediation guidance.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The terminal displays the issue report and its remediation guidance.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: Select what to scan: open-source dependencies, application code, a container, or infrastructure as code.
A Snyk account or company SSO invitation is required for the documented platform and authenticated CLI routes.
The CLI route requires a supported CLI installation; Open Source project scans may also require the project's package manager, build tools, and a prior build.

primary_path.action: Open Snyk's general getting-started page and identify the target system and Project type to scan.
details: Snyk says the required integration depends on the system, scan target, and Organization or Group scope.
Supported browsers require JavaScript; CLI paths require supported language and tooling.
primary_path.input: Developer security intent and a scan target.
primary_path.success_signal: No shared platform success signal exists at this choice point.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The journey branches before one implementation path can be reconstructed.

primary_path.action: Choose guided or manual SCM integration, or the local CLI route.
details: Guided setup can request integration method, access permissions, automation settings, and authentication.
CLI avoids source-repository authentication but requires installation and Snyk authentication.
primary_path.input: Human-selected scan target and operating context.
primary_path.output: A bounded official Snyk onboarding path.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected route can terminate at a documented scan report.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without this choice, account, permission, software, execution, and verification steps differ materially.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to the correct Snyk account and Organization, or complete the company's SSO invitation.
details: The authenticated CLI is associated with this account and Organization context.
primary_path.input: Snyk identity or organization SSO access.
primary_path.success_signal: The Snyk Web UI opens in the intended account context.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invitation, SSO, account, or Organization access must be resolved before CLI consent.

primary_path.action: Install the Snyk CLI using one documented method and make the `snyk` command available.
details: The npm route requires supported Node.js and npm; standalone, Homebrew, Yarn, Docker, and WSL have their own documented conditions.
primary_path.input: Supported local environment for the selected installer.
primary_path.success_signal: The `snyk` command can be invoked from the terminal.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Installer, runtime, path, permission, or WSL limitations require correcting the selected installation route.

primary_path.action: If the account is outside `SNYK-US-01`, run `snyk config environment <ENVIRONMENT>` with its documented regional value before authentication.
details: The default-region account skips this command.
primary_path.output: CLI configured for the account's Snyk environment.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI stores the correct regional environment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Using the wrong environment sends authentication to the wrong regional service and must be corrected before retrying.

primary_path.action: Run `snyk auth` to start the recommended OAuth authentication flow.
details: Personal access token and Snyk API token are documented alternatives.
primary_path.success_signal: The Snyk authentication page opens or its URL is provided.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Browser launch, network, region, or CLI errors prevent OAuth initiation.

primary_path.action: Log in if prompted, grant the Snyk CLI application access, complete browser confirmation, and return to the terminal.
details: The consent associates the local CLI with the signed-in Snyk identity.
primary_path.success_signal: The terminal prints `Your account has been authenticated` and regains control.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Denied consent, wrong account, SSO, expired browser flow, or organization policy requires restarting authentication.

primary_path.action: Run `snyk test ionic` to perform the documented vulnerability quick test against the public Ionic npm package.
details: Running `snyk --help` is the guide's alternative installation-only quick test, so it is not required in this vulnerability-report candidate.
A local project route instead builds when required, enters the manifest directory, and runs `snyk test`.
primary_path.output: CLI vulnerability test execution against the Ionic package.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI runs the Ionic vulnerability test instead of reporting installation or authentication failure.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Command, authentication, region, network, policy, or product-entitlement errors stop the scan.

details: The documented report includes found vulnerabilities, severity, description links, dependency paths, and fix guidance.
primary_path.success_signal: The terminal displays the issue report and its remediation guidance.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No report means the scan did not reach the candidate terminal and the preceding CLI error must be corrected before retrying.

branches.condition: The local CLI quick-test route is selected.
branches.path: Install Snyk CLI by one supported method. If the account is hosted outside the default `SNYK-US-01` environment, first run `snyk config environment <ENVIRONMENT>` with the documented regional value. Then run `snyk auth`, log in if prompted, grant app access, return to the terminal after the confirmation, run `snyk --help` or `snyk test ionic`, and inspect the terminal report. For a local Open Source Project, build it when required, enter the directory containing its supported manifest, and run `snyk test`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The first observable result is a CLI report listing issues and fix guidance, or a successful help display if installation-only verification is selected. Non-default-region accounts add an environment configuration gate before authentication.

branches.path: Create or log into the correct Snyk account and Organization, choose guided or manual integration, grant the documented repository permissions, configure automation and authenticate, import a Project, wait for Snyk to scan it, then review issue counts and severity-grouped results.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The first observable result is the imported Project's scan results in the Snyk Web UI.

friction_gates.description: Scan product and target type determine the rest of the path.
SSO provider, invitation, and Organization access can affect login and membership.
SCM integration can require repository access and automation permissions.
CLI route requires a supported installation and may require language-specific package managers, build tools, and a build.
Before `snyk auth`, accounts outside the default `SNYK-US-01` region must set the CLI environment with `snyk config environment`.
CLI must authenticate with recommended browser OAuth or another documented credential method.

sources.title: Getting started
sections_used: Create or log in to a Snyk account
Obtain and use your Snyk API token
Import a Project to scan and identify issues
Review results and fix your issues
evidence_supported: Repository import and scan result behavior

sources.title: Getting started with the Snyk CLI
sections_used: Install the Snyk CLI and authenticate your machine
Monitor your Open Source or Container Project

sources.title: Install or update the Snyk CLI
sections_used: Snyk CLI in a Docker image

sources.title: Authenticate to use the CLI
sections_used: How to authenticate to use the CLI locally

uncertainties.question: Which Snyk product, scan target, and integration interface represents the workshop participant's intended first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes prerequisites, permission gates, commands, result format, and whether the first result is local or uploaded.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Snyk's official general documentation explicitly makes the integration conditional and documents several scan products.

excluded_after_success: No shared after-success scope is selected while scan product and integration remain unresolved.
Monitoring, recurring scans, pull-request checks, remediation automation, policies, reporting, and enterprise rollout are excluded from the candidate first scan.
